worth_it_explanation: Account linking is the prerequisite for everything else in an Amazon DSP audit. A demand-side platform (DSP) advertiser that has not linked its Seller Central or Vendor Central account cannot receive retail purchase signals, cannot build 'new-to-brand' audiences, and cannot attribute sales back to campaign spend. The checks take minutes to complete in the Amazon DSP console and Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) setup screens. The consequences of a broken link are silent: campaigns run, spend accrues, and the attribution model shows nothing. The practitioner needs to confirm the connections are healthy before drawing any conclusions about campaign performance.
intro: Before Amazon DSP can attribute retail outcomes, the advertiser entity inside the DSP console must be linked to the Amazon retail accounts that hold the brand's product catalog and sales data. Endemic advertisers, those who sell products on Amazon, depend on a live Seller Central or Vendor Central link to count purchases, compute new-to-brand metrics, and build retail audiences. Non-endemic advertisers depend on the Amazon Ad Tag pixel to measure conversions that happen off Amazon. AMC adds a clean-room measurement layer on top, but it requires its own provisioning and linkage to be usable. These checks confirm that each connection exists, that it is in an active rather than pending or broken state, and that the right accounts are linked for the scope of the audit.

audit_checks.title: Amazon DSP advertiser is not linked to the brand's Seller Central or Vendor Central account
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without an active retail account link, Amazon DSP cannot receive purchase signals from Amazon.com. Retail conversion metrics, new-to-brand counts, and purchase-based audiences are all unavailable, so the account cannot measure whether campaigns drive product sales or reach customers who have not bought the brand before. The gap leaves the most valuable measurement outcomes the platform offers entirely dark.

educate.base:
Amazon DSP is built on Amazon's retail data graph. When an advertiser's DSP account is linked to a Seller Central or Vendor Central account, purchase events on Amazon.com flow back into the DSP's attribution model. The platform can then report purchases, detail page views, and 'new-to-brand' conversions (buyers who had not purchased from the brand in the prior twelve months) at the line-item level.

The link is established in the Amazon DSP console under the advertiser's account settings. It connects a specific Amazon Ads account (the container for the DSP advertiser entity) to a Seller Central account, a Vendor Central account, or both. A brand that sells through both first-party wholesale and third-party marketplace channels needs both linked to get a complete view of retail outcomes.

When the link is absent or in a 'pending' state, retail conversion columns in DSP reporting show dashes rather than numbers. Campaigns appear to have no attributed purchases, not because they are underperforming, but because the attribution path is broken. Audiences built from Amazon Standard Identification Number (ASIN) purchase history also become unavailable, so targeting that depends on past buyers or category shoppers cannot be created.

A few signs point to the link being missing or inactive:
  • Retail conversion columns in the DSP console show dashes or zeros across all campaigns and time ranges
  • Purchase-based audience segments are unavailable in audience creation
  • The advertiser entity settings page does not show a linked Seller Central or Vendor Central account
You may want to review the account settings before concluding that a campaign has no retail impact. The absence of reported purchases is often a linking problem rather than a performance problem.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: Endemic advertisers with an active store on Amazon.com gain the most from confirming this link. A Seller Central link makes third-party seller purchase data available in DSP reporting; a Vendor Central link makes first-party wholesale purchase data available. If the brand sells through both channels, you may want to confirm both accounts are linked so the attribution model reflects the full retail picture rather than only one sales channel.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Non-endemic advertisers driving to off-Amazon destinations do not depend on a Seller Central or Vendor Central link for their primary conversion measurement. The Amazon Ad Tag pixel handles off-site conversion tracking instead. You may want to skip this check for strictly non-endemic scopes and confirm the Ad Tag pixel is in place through the Ad Tag setup review.

learn_more.source_title: Manage all Amazon Ads products in one account

investigate.intro: Confirm that the DSP advertiser entity shows an active Seller Central or Vendor Central link and that retail conversion columns are populated in campaign reporting.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Log in to the Amazon DSP console and navigate to 'Advertiser' settings for the advertiser entity you are auditing.

Look for the linked Amazon Ads account section and note whether a Seller Central account, a Vendor Central account, or both are shown with an 'Active' status. A 'Pending' or absent entry means the link is not delivering retail data.

Open any active display or video campaign and view the performance table. Scan the 'Purchases', 'New-to-brand purchases', and 'Detail page views' columns. If all rows show dashes across multiple date ranges, the retail account link is not passing data even if an entry appears in settings.
If the brand operates in multiple countries, confirm that each marketplace (e.g., amazon.com, amazon.co.uk) has its own linked retail account. Retail data does not cross marketplace boundaries.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether a Seller Central account, a Vendor Central account, or both are linked with an 'Active' status in the advertiser entity settings, and whether retail conversion columns are populated in campaign reporting. An active link with non-zero retail conversion data for campaigns running against Amazon inventory passes. A missing link, a 'Pending' status, or a link that appears active but shows all-dash retail columns is a fail recorded with the advertiser entity name and the specific gap observed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the retail account link status and the retail conversion column observation for the advertiser entity above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP advertiser entity is not linked to an active Seller Central or Vendor Central account, which means retail purchase signals are not flowing into the DSP attribution model. Campaigns cannot report purchases, new-to-brand conversions, or detail page views, and purchase-based audiences are unavailable for targeting. {{voice_recommend}} completing the retail account linkage in the Amazon DSP console advertiser settings and confirming, after the link shows an 'Active' status, that retail conversion columns populate in campaign reporting. If the brand sells through both first-party wholesale and third-party marketplace channels, {{voice_recommend}} linking both account types so the attribution model reflects the full range of retail outcomes.

audit_checks.title: Amazon Marketing Cloud is not provisioned or is not linked to the DSP advertiser
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without an active Amazon Marketing Cloud instance, the advertiser has no access to multi-touch attribution, custom audience analysis, or path-to-purchase queries. The DSP console reports impression, click, and purchase totals, but it cannot answer questions about how campaigns work together across formats or channels. Measurement stays limited to last-touch, single-channel totals rather than the full signal the clean room is designed to provide.

educate.base:
Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) is a privacy-safe clean-room environment that Amazon Ads operates on behalf of each advertiser. It stores pseudonymized, event-level ad exposure and conversion records for up to 25 months and gives the advertiser a SQL-based interface to query those records without ever accessing individual-level data. The outputs are aggregated and anonymous; AMC enforces minimum group sizes and noise injection to prevent re-identification.

The practical value of AMC for an Amazon DSP audit is that it can answer questions the DSP console cannot: how many conversions required more than one touchpoint, which creative sequences produced the highest new-to-brand rate, and whether a streaming TV (STV) exposure followed by a display retargeting unit outperforms either format alone. Those questions require event-level joins across campaign types, which is exactly what the clean-room model supports.

AMC must be provisioned separately from the DSP console. The process involves requesting an AMC instance through the Amazon Ads account team or the AMC self-service portal, associating the instance with the DSP advertiser entity, and accepting the data processing terms. Provisioning can take days to weeks depending on the region and account type. A DSP advertiser that has never gone through this process has no AMC instance to query, regardless of how long the DSP account has been active.

A few signs point to AMC being unprovisioned or unlinked:
  • No AMC instance appears when the advertiser searches the AMC portal at the advertising.amazon.com domain
  • The Amazon Ads account team has no record of an AMC provisioning request for this advertiser entity
  • The DSP console's reporting interface does not surface an AMC audience or query entry point
You may want to raise AMC provisioning as a separate workstream from the DSP audit itself. It requires Amazon's involvement and has its own lead time.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For endemic advertisers running display, STV, and online video campaigns simultaneously, AMC provides the only path to understanding cross-format contribution to purchase. The clean room can join a display impression, an STV exposure, and an Amazon.com purchase into a single user path and report on conversion rates by touchpoint sequence. Without AMC, each format's contribution is reported in isolation, which overstates total reach and understates the share of sales that involved more than one format.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: Non-endemic advertisers who drive to off-Amazon destinations can use AMC to analyze the Amazon Ad Tag conversion data alongside DSP exposure records, giving a more complete picture of the path from DSP impression to off-site conversion. The value depends on the volume of off-site conversions the Ad Tag captures. For campaigns with low off-site conversion volume, the AMC query benefit may not justify the provisioning overhead.

learn_more.source_title: A complete guide to Amazon Marketing Cloud

investigate.intro: Confirm whether an AMC instance is provisioned for this advertiser and, if so, whether it is associated with the DSP advertiser entity currently under audit.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Ask the advertiser or account team whether an AMC instance has been requested and provisioned for this advertiser entity. Note the instance name or ID if one exists.

If an AMC instance exists, navigate to the AMC portal and confirm the instance is linked to the DSP advertiser entity under audit. An instance linked to a different advertiser entity will not surface this advertiser's campaign data.

Confirm the AMC data retention window. AMC retains up to 25 months of event-level data, but the instance only accumulates data from the date of provisioning. An instance provisioned recently will have limited history to query.
If no AMC instance is found, note the gap and flag it to the advertiser. AMC provisioning requires coordination with the Amazon Ads team and has a lead time that varies by region.

amazon_marketing_cloud.label: Amazon Marketing Cloud portal
steps: Log in to the AMC portal at advertising.amazon.com and search for instances associated with the advertiser's Amazon Ads account.
Confirm the instance status shows as active rather than pending or suspended.
Run a simple test query, such as a count of impressions over the past 30 days, to confirm the instance returns data for this advertiser's campaigns.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether an AMC instance is provisioned, whether it is linked to the correct DSP advertiser entity, and whether a test query returns data. An active instance linked to the correct advertiser with a successful test query passes. An instance that is missing, pending, linked to the wrong entity, or returning empty results is a fail recorded with the instance status and the gap observed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the AMC instance status and the test query result for this advertiser above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) is not provisioned or is not linked to {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP advertiser entity. Without AMC, measurement is limited to last-touch, single-channel totals from the DSP console, and the advertiser cannot analyze cross-format contribution, path-to-purchase sequences, or custom audience segments built from clean-room data. {{voice_recommend}} requesting AMC provisioning through the Amazon Ads account team and confirming, after the instance is active, that it is associated with the correct DSP advertiser entity and that a test query returns campaign data. The sooner the instance is provisioned, the sooner the 25-month event-level data window begins accumulating.

audit_checks.title: A linked account shows a broken or pending status, cutting off retail or measurement data without warning
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A link that shows an entry in the account settings but is not in an active state delivers the same data loss as a missing link, while appearing configured. The platform reports zeros or dashes, which can be misread as poor campaign performance rather than a broken connection. The advertiser may act on that misreading, pausing or restructuring campaigns that are actually working.

educate.base:
Account links in Amazon DSP are not a one-time configuration. They depend on the linked account remaining in good standing, the account owner's authorization persisting, and the link not expiring due to an inactivity timeout or a permission change on the linked account's side. A Seller Central account that is suspended, a Vendor Central account whose contact credentials have changed, or a Brand Registry connection whose trademark registration has lapsed can all cause the link to move from 'Active' to 'Pending' or 'Error' without any notification in the DSP console.

The symptom is the same in each case: retail conversion columns show dashes or zeros, purchase-based audiences become unavailable, and the account settings page shows the linked entry but with a non-active status indicator. Because the entry is still present, the DSP appears configured, and the gap can persist for weeks without anyone noticing.

A few signs point to a link being in a broken or pending state rather than simply absent:
  • The account settings page shows a Seller Central or Vendor Central entry but the status column reads 'Pending', 'Error', or 'Needs attention' rather than 'Active'
  • Retail conversion columns were populated in prior reporting periods but have shown dashes for the past several weeks
  • The advertiser recently changed the login credentials or permissions on the linked Seller Central or Vendor Central account
You may want to check the linked account's status in both the DSP console and within the linked Seller Central or Vendor Central account directly. The repair path depends on which end of the connection broke.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For endemic advertisers, a broken retail account link has immediate budget implications. If the DSP bidding strategy uses purchase-based audience signals and those signals stop flowing, the targeting narrows without warning. You may want to compare the audience reach figures in the weeks before and after retail conversion columns went dark to gauge whether the targeting pool has contracted.

learn_more.source_title: Manage all Amazon Ads products in one account

investigate.intro: Confirm the current status of every linked account entry in the Amazon DSP advertiser settings, and cross-reference with the retail conversion data trend in campaign reporting.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Navigate to the advertiser entity settings and open the linked accounts section. For each entry (Seller Central, Vendor Central, Brand Registry, or Amazon Ad Tag), read the status indicator and note any entry that is not 'Active'.

Open any active display or video campaign and pull a 90-day performance table. Look for a date range where retail conversion columns transitioned from populated to dashes. If a break point exists, record the approximate date.

If a link shows 'Pending', note how long it has been in that state. A link that remains 'Pending' for more than a few business days typically requires manual re-authorization from the linked account owner.

If a link shows 'Error' or 'Needs attention', contact the Amazon Ads account team with the error status and the linked account identifier so they can diagnose whether the issue is on the DSP side or the linked account side.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each linked account entry, the status shown in the advertiser settings and whether retail conversion columns are populated in current campaign reporting. An entry with an 'Active' status and populated retail columns passes. An entry with a 'Pending', 'Error', or 'Needs attention' status, or an entry that appears active but shows all-dash retail columns in recent reporting, is a fail recorded with the linked account type, the status shown, and the approximate date the data gap began.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the status of each linked account entry and the retail conversion column observation for recent campaigns above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more linked accounts in {{site_name}}'s Amazon DSP advertiser settings show a non-active status, cutting off retail conversion data or audience signals without surfacing a clear error. Retail conversion columns in campaign reporting show dashes where populated values appeared in prior periods, which suggests the break occurred after the account was initially configured. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing each linked account's status in the Amazon DSP advertiser settings, identifying which entry moved out of 'Active', and working with the Amazon Ads account team to restore the connection. If the linked Seller Central or Vendor Central account has changed ownership, credentials, or permissions recently, {{voice_recommend}} reauthorizing the link from within that account before expecting the DSP to resume receiving retail signals.
